analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet announces new EU priorities for cooperation with the Council of Europe on rule of law, highlighting work against corruption, protection of whistleblowers, and relevance to the EU Rule of Law mechanism, and invites participation in an information event. EU1 (successful story): Not explicitly about history or overcoming past obstacles; no. EU2 (unity): No mention of EU internal unity or solidarity; no. EU3 (useful for citizens): Yes—promotes concrete EU rule-of-law initiatives (anti-corruption, whistleblower protection) as practical measures that benefit citizens and invites public engagement, fitting the narrative that the EU addresses citizens’ concerns within the EU. EU4 (avant-garde): It does not claim pioneering leadership or first-mover regulation; weak/absent. EU5 (global champion of fair causes): Focus is internal EU/CoE cooperation on rule of law, not broad human rights leadership globally; no. EU6 (contributes to a better world): While it mentions cooperation with CoE (a multilateral partner), the emphasis is on internal EU mechanisms; insufficient to claim global contribution; no. Final: Only EU3 applies.
